nodes.label: Heatmap Tuning
nodes.rationale: Increase heatmap-intensity (2-6), heatmap-radius (25-50), heatmap-opacity (0.85) for better contrast and visibility of sparse demand gap clusters.

nodes.label: Visual Weight Property
nodes.rationale: Introduce visualWeight in GeoJSON features by clamping activeWeight to [1,20] to ensure low-weight nodes contribute enough pressure to the heatmap.

nodes.label: DemandGapLegend Component
nodes.rationale: Compact, absolute-positioned legend explaining color coding for three gap types (Uncaptured, Unserved, Unreachable Workplace), only visible when overlay is enabled.

nodes.label: Shell Panel Spacing and Inspector Disclosure Polish
nodes.norm_label: shell panel spacing and inspector disclosure polish

nodes.label: Shell CSS Tokens
nodes.rationale: Shared --openvayra-cities-shell-padding-x token to unify horizontal padding across left-panel, workspace, and right-panel.

nodes.label: Inspector Disclosure Polish
nodes.rationale: Reduce divider noise by keeping border-top on .inspector-disclosure for separation but minimizing redundant internal dividers.

nodes.label: focusedDemandGapId State
nodes.rationale: Ephemeral UI state in shell (App.tsx) storing ID of currently focused gap, separate from domain simulation state.

nodes.label: Demand Gap Overlay Focus Layer
nodes.rationale: Non-interactive circle layer (MAP_LAYER_ID_DEMAND_GAP_OVERLAY_FOCUS) rendering high-contrast white ring around focused gap, using filter ['==', ['get', 'focused'], true].

nodes.label: Demand Gap Planning Guidance
nodes.rationale: Category-specific guidance text for Uncaptured residential (stop placement), Captured but unserved (connect stops to destinations), Unreachable workplace (improve service reachability).

nodes.label: DemandGapOdContextProjection
nodes.rationale: Pure projection deriving likely OD candidates for focused demand gap, consuming scenario demand artifact and focused gap item without mutating domain state.

nodes.label: OD Context Deterministic Ranking
nodes.rationale: Candidates ranked by active time-band weight (descending), geographic distance (ascending), and stable ID (ascending) for determinism.

nodes.label: OD Context Candidate Cap
nodes.rationale: DEMAND_GAP_OD_CONTEXT_MAX_CANDIDATES = 5 to prevent UI clutter and unbounded iterations.

nodes.label: Demand Gap OD Desire Hint Map Context
nodes.norm_label: demand gap od desire hint map context

nodes.label: OD Desire Hints
nodes.rationale: Straight dashed contextual lines between focused demand gap and OD candidates, using MAP_DEMAND_GAP_OD_CONTEXT_HINT_PAINT subordinate style to distinguish from transit lines.

nodes.label: demandGapOdContextGeoJson Builder
nodes.rationale: Pure MapLibre GeoJSON builder deriving LineString features from DemandGapOdContextProjection without calculating demand, routing, or passenger assignment.

nodes.label: demand-gap-od-context Layer
nodes.rationale: Registered in map layer flyout as Demand gap hints, default visibility false to avoid map clutter.

nodes.label: Focused Demand Gap Planning Summary Projection
nodes.norm_label: focused demand gap planning summary projection

nodes.label: focusedDemandGapPlanningProjection
nodes.rationale: Pure projection deriving compact planning summary (title, primaryAction, supportingContext, caveat, evidence) from DemandGapRankingProjection and DemandGapOdContextProjection.

nodes.label: Planning Summary Evidence
nodes.rationale: Deterministic evidence facts: active pressure, nearest stop, OD candidates count, grounding guidance in simulation truth.

nodes.label: Demand Gap OD Candidate Focus and Readability
nodes.norm_label: demand gap od candidate focus and readability

nodes.label: DemandGapOdCandidateListProjection
nodes.rationale: Dedicated projection consuming DemandGapOdContextProjection, mapping raw candidates to typed display rows (DemandGapOdCandidateDisplayRow) with ordinal labels and formatted weights/distances.

nodes.label: Candidate Map-Focus Action
nodes.rationale: Map-focus action on each candidate row dispatching onPositionFocus intent with candidate coordinates.

nodes.label: Focused Demand Gap Planning Action Entrypoints
nodes.norm_label: focused demand gap planning action entrypoints

nodes.label: Coverage Gap Stop Placement Entrypoint
nodes.rationale: Action transitions workspace to place-stop mode and focuses map on gap position for coverage gaps.

nodes.label: Connectivity Gap Build Line Entrypoint
nodes.rationale: Action transitions workspace to build-line mode and focuses map on gap position for connectivity/reachability gaps.

nodes.label: Demand Gap Planning Entrypoint Context Banner
nodes.norm_label: demand gap planning entrypoint context banner

nodes.label: Planning Context Banner State
nodes.rationale: Transient shell-owned state set when planning entrypoint triggered, cleared on dismiss, demand gap focus clear, or manual mode switch to neutral.

nodes.label: Focused Demand Gap Lifecycle Projection
nodes.rationale: Pure projection deriving status (unfocused, active, not-currently-ranked) from focusedDemandGapId and DemandGapRankingProjection output without recomputing demand math.

nodes.label: Scenario Demand Provenance and Model Caveat
nodes.norm_label: scenario demand provenance and model caveat

nodes.label: Scenario Demand Provenance Projection
nodes.rationale: Pure projection deriving player-facing labels from ScenarioDemandArtifact metadata: generator name/version, contributing source materials, licensing hints. Runtime consumes artifact metadata only, not raw source files.

nodes.label: Demand Model Caveat
nodes.rationale: UI explicitly states demand is generated for gameplay planning context, not observed passenger demand or true OD matrix. Stops capture/serve demand but do not generate it.

nodes.label: DemandMapLegend Component
nodes.rationale: Refactored from DemandGapLegend, handles both demand gaps and OD context hints with mandatory caveat: Generated scenario demand. Planning context only.

nodes.label: Non-Claim Wording Policy
nodes.rationale: Player-facing copy avoids precision claims (actual demand, real flow, true OD), uses Demand gap pressure and Straight planning hints. OD hints labeled as not routed geometries.

nodes.label: Heatmap Visual Softening
nodes.rationale: Reduce heatmap-opacity (0.85 to 0.6), heatmap-intensity (2-6 to 1.1-2.6), subdue heatmap-color ramp with lower alpha (peak 0.6), adjust heatmap-radius (30-58).

nodes.label: Gap Circle Visual Softening
nodes.rationale: Subdue semantic colors (uncaptured #991b1b, unserved #9a3412, unreachable #5b21b6), lower circle-radius to 4, circle-opacity to 0.5, circle-stroke-opacity to 0.55.

nodes.label: Demand Node Focus and Time-Band Inspection
nodes.norm_label: demand node focus and time-band inspection

nodes.label: selectedDemandNodeId State
nodes.rationale: Shell-owned state for selected demand node, separate from focusedDemandGapId. Clicking scenario demand node on map selects it.

nodes.label: inspectDemandTimeBandSelection State
nodes.rationale: UI-only override state defaulting to follow-simulation but settable to any canonical time band, affecting only inspection projection without disrupting simulation clock.

nodes.label: demandNodeInspectionProjection
nodes.rationale: Pure projection consuming scenario demand artifacts, providing node metadata, time-band active weight, capture/service status, and likely context candidates (workplaces for origins, residential for destinations).

nodes.label: Demand Node Context Hints Map Rendering
nodes.rationale: Selected demand node context candidates rendered as straight planning hints using same visual style as demand gap OD hints. Node context hints take priority over demand gap hints.
nodes.norm_label: demand node context hints map rendering

nodes.label: Hyperbolic Distance Decay Scoring
nodes.rationale: contextScore = activeWeight / (1 + distanceMeters / DEMAND_NODE_CONTEXT_DISTANCE_DECAY_METERS) to rank candidates by locality-aware weight instead of global weight-first sorting.

nodes.label: DEMAND_NODE_CONTEXT_DISTANCE_DECAY_METERS
nodes.rationale: Set to 4000m. 4km retains 50% weight, 8km ~33%, 12km ~25%. Prevents hints from collapsing into neighborhood-only context while penalizing extreme distances.

nodes.label: Selected Demand Node Service Coverage Projection
nodes.norm_label: selected demand node service coverage projection

nodes.label: Service Coverage Logic
nodes.rationale: Explains stop-access radius coverage, nearby covering stops, completed lines with covering stops, structural connectivity to opposite-side candidates, and active frequency service in selected inspection time band.

nodes.label: Selected Demand Node Service Coverage Map Highlights
nodes.norm_label: selected demand node service coverage map highlights

nodes.label: Demand Service Coverage Map Overlay
nodes.rationale: Pure GeoJSON point projection and MapLibre circle ring layer highlighting selected-side and opposite-side covering stops, registered in map layer flyout as Demand service coverage.

nodes.label: OSM Stop Candidate Inspection and Adoption Readiness
nodes.norm_label: osm stop candidate inspection and adoption readiness

nodes.label: OSM Stop Candidate Inspection Projection
nodes.rationale: Pure projection for selected OSM candidate group, resolving transient street-anchor readiness and rendering player-facing readiness copy. Clearly states OSM candidates are not canonical stops until adopted.

nodes.label: OSM Candidate Adoption Readiness
nodes.rationale: Readiness based on resolved street-anchor and duplicate-stop checks, not on service, demand, or simulation truth. Candidate is not a game stop until explicitly adopted.

nodes.label: Census Grid Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Multi-stage pipeline: External Census Data -> prepare-census CLI -> Normalized CSV -> Local Manifest JSON -> build-scenario-demand CLI -> Demand Artifact JSON. Uses EPSG:3035 to WGS84 (EPSG:4326) transformation via proj4.

nodes.label: Census Data Sources
nodes.rationale: Eurostat GISCO Population Grids (1km EU grid cells) and German Zensus 2022 (high-density structural demographics). Users responsible for license verification.

nodes.label: OSM Attractor Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Raw OSM .osm.pbf -> Docker osmium extract -> Intermediate GeoJSON -> prepare-osm-attractors CLI -> Normalized GeoJSON -> Local Manifest -> build-scenario-demand CLI -> Demand Artifact JSON.

nodes.label: OSM Attractor Tag Categories
nodes.rationale: Classified tags: education (school/college/university), health (hospital/clinic), civic (townhall), workplace-office, retail, leisure-tourism, workplace-industrial. Weights and scales assigned by category.

nodes.label: OSM Attractor Licensing
nodes.rationale: OSM data under Open Database License (ODbL). Do not bundle unapproved upstream binaries in repositories.

nodes.label: OSM Stop Candidate Consolidation
nodes.rationale: Grouping within 35m for compatible labels, 5m for exact duplicates, max group span 60m. Passenger-visible objects grouped first, then vehicle stop positions assigned to best nearby group.

nodes.label: OSM Stop Street Anchor Resolution
nodes.rationale: Display position favors passenger-visible platforms/signs; routing anchor favors OSM stop-position nodes. Street anchor status: Ready (<=35m), Review (<=60m), Blocked (>60m or none).

nodes.label: OSM Stop Adoption Workflow
nodes.rationale: Selection in Inspect mode -> Inspector shows street anchor/distance -> Adopt if ready. Adopted stop becomes canonical, uses resolved street anchor position, inherits candidate group label.

nodes.label: OSRM RoutingAdapter Interface
nodes.rationale: Local Dockerized OSRM instance provides deterministic street routing between stops. Uses car.lua profile as bus routing approximation. Replaceable adapter interface for future engine swaps.

nodes.label: OSRM Graph Preparation Pipeline
nodes.rationale: prepare-osrm.ps1 mounts data directory into osrm-backend Docker container, runs osrm-extract, osrm-partition, osrm-customize. Graph artifacts placed in data/routing/osrm/<areaId>/.

hyperedges.label: Stop Selection to Inspector Projection Flow
Route segment domain and fallback routing chain
Selected-line export schema, validation, and fixture pipeline
Session Loader and Map Sync Lifecycle
Map Style Readiness and Data Sync
